product_overview: A Streamlit-based pricing assistant that scrapes competitor catalogs, compares them to your store catalog, and recommends pricing actions with optional AI-assisted verification and export/webhook integration.
core_goals: Collect competitor product data reliably with progress, checkpointing, and resumable scraping.
Compare the store catalog to competitors and classify products into pricing actions (lower, raise, approve, defer, missing).
Provide AI-assisted verification and deep review to improve match confidence and automate reclassification where safe.
Enable exports and automated delivery of pricing decisions to external systems (Make.com/webhooks).
Surface errors and limits (scraping rate limits, missing API keys) and allow users to recover or retry.

features.name: Competitor Scraping Pipeline
features.description: Scrape competitor sitemaps and product pages with live progress indicators, checkpointing, and resumable runs so users can gather competitor pricing and product data.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Paste or select competitor sitemap/store URLs -> Start scraping and analysis -> Watch live scraping progress and row counters -> See scraping complete and products persisted to local DB

Navigate to / -> Paste or select competitor sitemap/store URLs -> Start scraping and analysis -> See scraping stalls or errors due to target site rate limits or anti-bot behavior -> Observe partial progress and checkpoint saved -> Adjust input URLs or retry scraping

features.name: Price Comparison and Decision Workflow
features.description: Upload your catalog, compare it against scraped competitor data, classify each product into decision groups (higher, lower, approved, missing, review), and accept or modify suggested pricing actions before exporting or pushing them to external systems.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Upload own catalog CSV -> Provide or confirm competitor data is available -> Run analysis and review sections -> Inspect grouped results (higher, lower, approved, missing, review) -> Approve or modify decisions per product -> Export decisions to CSV/Excel or push approved decisions to external integration

Navigate to / -> Upload own catalog CSV missing required identifier column -> Run analysis -> See validation error indicating missing 'no' identifier required for exports/webhooks -> Add or map 'no' column in catalog -> Re-run analysis

features.name: AI-Assisted Verification and Deep Analysis
features.description: Use Gemini/OpenRouter-backed AI utilities to verify matches, reclassify ambiguous items, run deep market checks, and interact via chat for bulk or single-product investigations.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Trigger AI review actions for review items -> Run automatic reclassification for selected items -> See AI verification results with confidence scores -> Accept AI suggestions to reclassify and persist decisions

Navigate to / -> Trigger AI review actions -> See AI unavailable error due to missing or invalid GEMINI_API_KEYS -> Add GEMINI_API_KEYS via secrets or environment variables -> Retry AI review and receive verification results

routes.description: Main Streamlit interface for scraping, matching, and pricing decisions

features.name: Competitor Scraping Pipeline
features.description: Scrape competitor sitemaps and product pages with progress and checkpointing
user_interactions: Paste or select competitor sitemap/store URLs
Watch live scraping progress and row counters

api_calls.endpoint: Competitor sitemap and product page URLs

features.name: Price Comparison and Decision Workflow
features.description: Compare catalog products against competitors and classify actions
user_interactions: Approve, lower, raise, or defer decisions per product

features.name: AI-Assisted Verification and Deep Analysis
features.description: Use Gemini/OpenRouter-backed checks for reclassification and deep review
user_interactions: Trigger AI review actions and deep analysis buttons
Run automatic reclassification for review items
Use AI chat and verification utilities

known_limitations.issue: No built-in user authentication; app access is open by default
known_limitations.impact: Anyone with app URL can use scraping and pricing tools

known_limitations.issue: Heavy scraping depends on target site rate limits and anti-bot behavior
known_limitations.impact: Coverage and speed may vary across stores
